entry_a.title: Issue work happens in per-issue git worktrees
entry_a.content: Every issue gets its own worktree under the workspace_base directory. The main repo checkout is never modified. This lets multiple agents work concurrently without branch-switching collisions.

entry_b.title: Never modify the primary checkout
entry_b.content: All issue-scoped changes must land in a dedicated worktree — branch protection on main enforces this. Worktrees are created/destroyed per issue lifecycle so parallel runs don't collide on branch state.

notes: Both advise per-issue worktree isolation and non-modification of the main checkout.
